Digital product leadership

Demonstrated experience enhancing and maintaining customer-facing digital products
Hands-on experience with Agile, Scrum, Lean, CX and UX delivery methodologies
Strong facilitation, coaching and motivational skills with cross-functional teams
High-level analytical and problem-solving skills, with confidence using data to inform decisions

IT Auditor Specialist

Company: BGIS
Employment Type: Full Time (12 Month Contract)
Location: Open to applicants across Australia

About the Role

We are seeking a motivated IT Audit & Compliance Analyst to support and strengthen our IT General Controls (ITGC) and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) compliance program across APAC. This is a great opportunity to build your expertise in cybersecurity, information security frameworks, and risk management within a dynamic, multi-sector environment.

In this role, you will help monitor control effectiveness, support audit activities, and drive continuous improvement in our compliance posture. You will also work closely with senior leadership, external auditors, and cross-functional teams, making strong stakeholder management and communication skills essential.

Key Responsibilities

Controls Testing, Documentation & Compliance Management

Support the IT Risk & Controls Manager in monitoring compliance with internal IT policies and external regulatory requirements, including SOX and ISO 27001
Assist in the execution and documentation of IT control testing procedures to evaluate control design and effectiveness, identifying potential gaps
Perform control testing in line with established methodologies and frameworks such as SOX ITGC and ISO 27001
Maintain accurate, well-organised documentation of testing activities, results, and supporting evidence to ensure audit readiness
Assist in preparing audit observations, control deficiencies, and risk findings for internal review prior to stakeholder communication
Support the development of corrective and preventive action plans aligned with business objectives
Track remediation actions from internal and external audits, following up with control owners to ensure timely closure and evidence collection
Support audit engagements by collating and providing audit evidence to internal and external auditors in a timely manner
Provide guidance to control owners on execution requirements, documentation standards, and testing expectations
Identify opportunities to improve audit efficiency and control testing processes
Contribute to IT risk register maintenance and compliance reporting metrics
Stay informed on regulatory requirements, industry standards, and audit practices
What We're Looking For

We are looking for candidates who bring both strong technical capability in SOX/ITGC environments and the ability to operate confidently in stakeholder-facing situations. Many applicants typically demonstrate one of these strengths, but success in this role requires both.

Essential Experience
2+ years of experience in IT audit, IT controls testing, or information security compliance
2+ years of hands-on SOX compliance experience, including Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 ITGC testing
Demonstrated experience with frameworks such as SOX, COBIT, COSO, or ITIL
Proven ability to conduct control walkthroughs, design test procedures, and document testing evidence
Professional Skills
Strong stakeholder management skills, with experience engaging senior leadership, external auditors, and cross-functional teams
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear, business-friendly language
Strong reporting capabilities, including preparation of audit findings, risk insights, and executive-ready documentation
High proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
Qualifications

Required

Bachelor's degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Accounting, Information Security, or a related field

Certifications (Preferred or In Progress)

CISA, CIA, or ISO 27001 Lead Auditor / Lead Implementer
CISSP, CISM, CRISC, or other relevant cybersecurity certifications
Why Join BGIS?

✔ AFR Best Places to Work 2024 & 2025
✔ Award-winning DE&I culture (Work180 Accredited)
✔ Leadership development & internal career mobility
✔ Health & wellbeing programs including EAP & flu vaccinations
✔ 2 paid volunteer days each year + donation matching
✔ Recognition programs & innovation initiatives
✔ Global organisation with 12,000+ people and 50,000+ sites
✔ Sustainability-focused workplace

BGIS is an equal opportunity employer. We strongly encourage applications from women, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, LGBTQ+ individuals, and people from culturally diverse backgrounds.

Apply now to take the next step in your career with BGIS.

About Us:

Blackbook.ai is an Australian founded company specialising in Artificial Intelligence, Automation, DataOps, Digital and Analytics. We are passionate about helping organisations unlock their true value, with a proven track record across more than 180 successful client engagements.

Focused on leveraging and nurturing local talent, we provide Australian companies with access to skilled consultants who deliver innovative solutions to complex business challenges. Headquartered in Brisbane with additional offices in Sydney and Melbourne, Blackbook.ai now operates across more than 8 cities globally, collaborating with prominent organisations to drive efficiency, excellence, and measurable outcomes through cutting-edge technology.

About the Team Your are Joining:

You'll be joining our GenAI and Applied AI team. Our focus is building production AI solutions for large Australian enterprises and government clients, with most of our work centred on GenAI and applied AI: RAG systems, document intelligence, agents, and LLM-backed workflows that need to hold up under real data volumes, security requirements, and operational load. We take AI from prototype to production. That means hardening, scaling, deploying, and handing over systems clients can actually run, along with greenfield delivery end-to-end when clients bring us the problem rather than the POC.

About You:

Senior, hands-on delivery role within the GenAI / Applied AI team, leading production GenAI work for large Australian enterprise and government clients. You'd architect, build, and deploy RAG systems, agents, document intelligence, and LLM-backed workflows — typically taking a POC/MVP to production (security, scale, observability, evaluation) or leading greenfield delivery end-to-end, while owning the client relationship through scoping, build, and handover. Also mentors juniors and contributes to internal patterns/accelerators.

Must have: 5+ years across SWE/data/ML/AI with the last 12–24 months in production GenAI; strong Python + API/service dev; production experience on Azure and/or AWS; consulting delivery chops (scoping, workshops, stakeholder management); daily use of AI coding tools; CS/SWE/DS degree or equivalent; full Australian working rights.

Nice to have: regulated-industry delivery, Databricks/GCP, agent frameworks (LangGraph, Semantic Kernel, AutoGen), eval-led development, full-stack/DevOps breadth.

Location: Brisbane preferred; Sydney/Melbourne considered. Some travel to client sites.

What We Offer:

Applied AI work with large Australian enterprises and government clients, on problems that go into production.

A team that takes production quality seriously: testing, evaluation, observability, and proper handover.

Direct client engagement. You'll be working with clients, not relaying tickets.

Investment in certifications (Azure, AWS, Databricks) and time for internal R&D.

Competitive remuneration, commensurate with experience.

Flexible working conditions and a collaborative, technically strong team.

Free Spotify Accounts

50% off Gym memberships

Additional leave entitlements after 2 years

Make a difference everyday

Join HammondCare and help improve quality of life for older Australians in need. As a not-for-profit, Christian organisation, we provide expert aged care, home care, dementia care and palliative care. Mission-led, we build passionate teams who support older people through life's most difficult circumstances.



With over 90 years of experience, we're known for our relationship-based approach. Whether it's in our homes, hospitals or the community, we bring skill, empathy and innovation to every moment of care.



It's not just care. It's HammondCare.



About The Role

At HammondCare, data and information are critical to delivering safe, high quality care, maintaining trust with the people we serve, and enabling responsible innovation.

The Data Governance Lead is accountable for leading the design, implementation, and ongoing operation of HammondCare's enterprise data governance framework, ensuring data and information assets are trusted, high‑quality, secure, compliant, and ethically managed across the organisation.



This role translates HammondCare's enterprise data and information management strategy into practical, fit‑for‑purpose governance standards, processes, and controls. Working closely with clinical, operational, technology, analytics, privacy, and risk stakeholders, the Data Governance Lead embeds governance into day to day operations, decision making, and change initiatives enabling teams to use data confidently while meeting regulatory, privacy, and ethical obligations.



This is a hands on leadership role focused on stewardship, clarity, and adoption, ensuring governance enables care, insight, and innovation rather than slowing it down.



This is a permanent full-time role working at St Leonards with hybrid opportunities.



What we can offer you

We care about your wellbeing at HammondCare and as a result offer a wide range of health and lifestyle benefits including:


NFP salary packaging ($15,900 tax free) plus meals and entertainment benefit (up to $2,650 tax free)



Additional leave purchase options and 14 weeks paid parental leave



Flexible working options



Discounts on everyday products and services



Discounted healthcare memberships - access to network of fitness centres and pools for employees and families



Free access to independent counselling support for employees and families



Health & Wellbeing programmes



Reward & Recognition programmes



Referral bonuses



Career and professional development opportunities



Key Outcomes


Consistent and embedded enterprise data governance practices across diverse clinical, operational, and corporate environments

Data that is accessible and usable for care, operations, and insight, while meeting privacy, security, ethical, and regulatory obligations

Strong organisational adoption of governance standards through influence, collaboration, and clear decision‑making, without reliance on direct line authority

Improved data quality, ownership, and accountability across multiple systems, platforms, and data sources, reducing risk and rework



Responsibilities


Design, implement, and operate HammondCare's enterprise data governance framework, including standards, roles, decision rights, and escalation pathways

Establish and embed data quality management practices across key clinical, operational, and corporate data domains

Partner with privacy, risk, cyber, and legal teams to ensure compliance with privacy, security, ethical, and regulatory requirements

Define and maintain metadata, data classification, and information lifecycle standards to support discoverability, access control, retention, and defensible data management

Engage and influence clinical, operational, and technology stakeholders to adopt governance practices through collaboration and enablement, not mandate

Develop governance reporting, metrics, and maturity measures to monitor adoption, identify risks, and drive continuous improvement



About you


Demonstrated experience in data governance, data quality, or information management within complex, regulated environments

Tertiary qualifications in Information Management, Data, Analytics, Information Systems, or a related discipline (or equivalent practical experience)

Strong understanding of data governance frameworks, privacy principles, and information security concepts

Experience working with data platforms, analytics, and reporting environments to embed governance controls into everyday use

Proven ability to influence, collaborate, and drive change across multidisciplinary stakeholder groups without direct authority

Strong analytical, problem‑solving, and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly

Relevant professional certifications in data governance, privacy, or information management are desirable but not essential



Our Culture

HammondCare is committed to creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity and inclusion. We strive to create an equal employment environment for all.


To Apply

If you are ready to make a difference with HammondCare, Apply Now!

Position environment: 

Incolink provides a safety net for workers in the commercial building and construction industry where permanency and continuity of employment are significant issues. Our members get the security of redundancy payments, portable sick leave, income protection insurance and industry-best training. Through sound investment of member entitlements, we fund health and wellbeing supports like counselling and mental health services to build a stronger industry and help workers when they need us most. We are Australia’s largest provider of severance benefits and income protection, and a true employer and union partnership.  


Purpose of position:  

Incolink is reviewing its bespoke iLink platform along with overall technology stack seeking to create a modern architecture built to futureproof our core business functions and streamline our processes. The Business Analyst will work closely with the Solutions Architect and business stakeholders to map, analyse, and validate existing system logic processes, legal rules, and system behaviours. You will play a critical role in ensuring business continuity, legislative compliance, and clarity of scope throughout the modernisation journey. 

This role is suited to someone experienced in analysing complex, regulatory-driven systems, ideally within insurance, superannuation, or similar, who can bridge the gap between legacy logic and a potential modern platform capability.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Lead the discovery and documentation of current state processes, workflows, data structures, and business rules underpinning the iLink platform.

Partner with the Solutions Architect to define the target-state operating model and process improvements aligned to Power Platform and Dataverse capabilities.

Facilitate workshops and interviews with internal teams

Translate business and regulatory requirements into detailed functional specs, process maps, and user stories.

Identify system pain points, opportunities for simplification, and modules suitable for early migration.

Support UAT planning, traceability matrices, and documentation of acceptance criteria.

Track and log changes to business rules and ensure regulatory requirements are met post-transition.

BAU Projects – data archival, purging, process improvement, system upgrades if any. 

 

Technical Expertise 

5+ years' experience in Business Analyst roles, ideally in enterprise transformation or system replacement projects

Strong documentation skills (process flows, use cases, data dictionaries, BRDs, functional specs)

Comfortable reverse-engineering undocumented legacy logic from code and stakeholder input

Familiarity with structured data models and relational databases (SQL, ERDs)

Proficient with tools such as:

Visio, Lucidchart or BPMN modelling tools

DevOps, Jira, Confluence, Miro

Excel for mapping data logic or workflows

Experience with:

Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse)

Dynamics 365 CRM

Azure App Services, Functions, API Management.




Desirable:

Familiarity with compliance obligations (CPS234, Essential 8, ISO27001)

Experience supporting or analysing legacy applications in .NET / C++

Knowledge of API specs and understanding of REST/JSON format

Ability to write user stories for Power Apps development




Personal Attributes:

Curious, analytical, and methodical

Skilled at translating between technical and non-technical audiences

Comfortable working in ambiguity and reverse-engineering logic

Collaborative and empathetic – able to balance IT, regulatory and business needs

Ability to obtain a clean National Police Check.

 

Contract Type:

12-month initial contract with potential for extension.

Senior Financial Analyst

Drive business excellence at one of Australia’s northernmost coal ports

Play a key role in shaping business outcomes across the terminal

Join a collaborative, high-performance team

For more than 40 years, the North Qld Export Terminal, located at the Port of Abbot Point, has demonstrated its commitment to safety, efficiency, responsibility, and partnership with the local community.

APO has been contracted by North Queensland Export Terminal Pty Ltd to manage operations of its coal export terminal at the Port of Abbot Point - Australia’s northernmost coal port - located 25 kilometres north of Bowen, between Townsville and Mackay, serving as the gateway to the Whitsundays.

Abbot Point Operations is seeking an experienced and commercially astute Senior Financial Analyst to join our Finance team based in Bowen, North Queensland. Reporting to the Finance Manager, you will play a critical role in delivering high-quality financial insights, supporting strategic decision-making, and driving business performance across a complex, multi-disciplinary operation.

This is a unique opportunity to work in one of Australia’s key export terminals, located at the gateway to the Whitsundays, while contributing to a high-performing and collaborative team.

What you will do

In this broad and impactful role, you will be responsible for financial, management, and project accounting activities, while partnering closely with operational teams to optimise business outcomes.

Leading budgeting and forecasting processes (annual, quarterly, and monthly cycles)

Performing detailed financial analysis, modelling, and variance reporting

Managing cost control activities and identifying performance improvement opportunities

Preparing and delivering management and operational reports for key stakeholders

Supporting treasury and cashflow management

Driving process improvements, automation, and financial planning efficiencies

Participating in audits, governance, and compliance activities

Building strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders

Providing strategic recommendations to enhance financial performance and reduce risk

What we're looking for

You are a commercially minded finance professional with strong analytical capability and experience operating in complex, site-based or industrial environments.

Minimum 5  years’ experience in a similar senior finance role

Degree in Accounting, Finance, Commerce, or Business

CPA or CA qualification (preferred)

Strong expertise in budgeting, forecasting, and financial modelling

Experience managing large budgets and cost control frameworks

Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills

Proven ability to partner with stakeholders across multiple departments

Experience with ERP systems (SAP highly regarded)

Background in resources, heavy industry, or large-scale operations (preferred)

Why Join Us?

Competitive remuneration package

Career development and progression opportunities

Remote Area Housing Salary Sacrifice benefits

Work-life balance in a stunning coastal location

Be part of a business with over 35 years of operational excellence

Analytics Engineer


Supp is a fast-growing jobs platform connecting over 200,000 hospitality professionals with more than 8,000 independent venues across Australia and the US. By harnessing real-time data and technology, we're tackling some of the industry's toughest staffing challenges - making life easier for both workers and venues. As we continue to grow, we're looking for talented people to join us and help shape the future of hospitality.

We're looking for an Analytics Engineer to work within our ML and Analytics team - someone skilled at helping others extract meaningful insight from data and make a real impact on the future of our company.

In this position, you'll work across our BigQuery Data Mesh and BI systems, helping create and maintain a core suite of reports, and maintaining the DBT pipelines that fuel our BI, and ML systems. You'll also help drive our Data Platform, ensuring that our data is secure, high quality, and ready to be accessed efficiently to guide business decisions.

We are not looking for an Excel specialist, but someone with experience with fully-fledged Data Platforms and is comfortable with BI systems such as Data Studio, or Tableau.

About the platform

Supp is a dynamic two-sided marketplace connecting businesses and hospitality professionals in real time.

We use data-driven matching to pair the right candidates with the right opportunities.

More than just a jobs board, we have created a platform that offers management of worker profiles, business listings, payments, reviews & ratings, administration and community.

We've only scratched the surface of what's possible. Our growth has given us a clear view of the huge opportunities ahead - and we're building fast to make them happen.

The way we work
Size

As a small team, we hire productive, capable people so that we can stay lean and efficient. Fewer meetings, fewer committees. More time building for our customers!

Teamwork

As a company we strive to make data-driven decisions. You'll collaborate across the business to deliver insights that shape our product, and enable confident decisions

Empowerment

As a team that values each individual's input, we want everyone to bring their ideas to the table. Your expertise will be invaluable in shaping our data platform, and reporting systems

Location

We are a fully distributed team. While we prefer candidates within 2–3 hours of the Australia/Melbourne timezone for smoother teamwork, exceptional applicants from further afield are welcome to apply.

Part-time friendly

Have kids or want to work less and play more? We're open to people who want to work between 3 to 5 days per week.

Successful candidates
What You'll Do

Build and document production-grade ELT data pipelines, following a Medallion architecture

Maintain a core set of BI reports, enabling data-driven decisions by the business

Collaborate with Data Scientists, Engineers to deliver high-impact data products

Help guide our Data Platform, Data Governance, and Data Catalogue tooling. Ensuring data is accessible, secure, high quality, and in an efficient structure

Work with the wider Engineering Team to ensure that data is recorded, and stored efficiently in Iceberg formats

Help improve our Data Platform to bring improved federated data access, empowering the company to derive data-driven insights without waiting

Our existing Data Mesh architecture is hosted on GCP, utilizing Bigquery, and DBT Pipelines, interfacing with downstream ML Pipelines, and Looker Studio Reports

What You'll Bring

Comprehensive expertise in advanced SQL techniques including window functions, CTEs, and query optimisation

Hands-on experience building and maintaining Data Architectures, Data Pipelines, BI Systems, and Semantic Layers within Data Platforms

Familiarity with applicable technologies( BigQuery, IAM, etc ), particularly within Google Cloud Platform

Experience in Report Design

Experience in Python ( especially Pandas, Polars, PySpark ) is a plus

Posting Statement

At Supp, we believe that a small way we can improve the world is by striving for equality. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. If you require assistance at any stage of the application process, please let us know.

Applying

If you feel like this could be the next exciting step in your career, get in touch! We look forward to meeting you!

Job Title: IFS Enterprise Data Stream Lead | NSW Gov | $1344 per Day
Ad Text:
Highly sought after Government Organisation

9 months contract | Mid-May start

Experience in ERP and Mobility Services Program/ IFS systems are highly regarded

Our client is a large Government organisation who are seeking an experienced Enterprise Data Stream Lead to join their team.



In this role, you will be the accountable person to own, shape and deliver all the work products relating to the data stream, this includes data architecture, data governance, data migration, data archiving, and reporting & analytics.  The role provides a single point of accountability for the Data Stream, ensuring data solutions support program objectives, enterprise standards, regulatory obligations, and business outcomes. The role operates as both a strategic leader and a pragmatic delivery lead within a complex transformation environment.





Key Responsibilities: 



Lead and deliver the end-to-end data stream and own the overall Data Stream scope, roadmap, milestones, and dependencies.

Define and govern the target and transitional data architectures and oversee conceptual, logical, and physical data models aligned to business processes and program outcomes.

Establish and embed fit for purpose data governance practices, own the data migration, data archiving and retention strategies, and prioritise reporting and analytics requirements required to support program and business outcomes.

Selection Criteria:



10+ year's experience leading data streams within large scale transformation or enterprise programs. with strong delivery focus across data engineering, governance, and analytics.

Strong background in data architecture, data migration, and data governance with demonstrated ability to manage cross stream dependencies and delivery risk.

Experience supporting ERP and Mobility Services Program/ IFS systems within regulated or complex enterprise environments are highly regarded

On offer is a 9 month contract at a highly sought after government organisation, with a highly competitive daily rate.

Category:
Product Management & Development (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About the ABC 

The ABC is the nation's most trusted and independent source of Australian conversations, culture, and stories. With over 4,000 employees from diverse backgrounds across over 50 locations around Australia and overseas, we are proud to create, curate and deliver high-quality content that informs, educates and entertains Australian communities 

About the Role 

As Product Manager, Audience Reengagement you will be responsible for shaping product strategy and delivery, while driving technology and capability enhancements across key ABC marketing, media and related audience reengagement tools and platforms. 

Working within a cross-functional team you will lead the technical management and optimisation of the ABC’s Marketing Automation Platform and CRM capabilities, within the broader ABC technology ecosystem of data, personalisation, recommendations, identity and audience facing products. You will contribute to roadmap development and be responsible for translating priorities into clear, actionable backlogs and delivery plans.  

You will work closely with Marketing, Content and Data stakeholders to identify opportunities to improve audience engagement and retention, ensuring initiatives are informed by data and aligned to organisational objectives. This includes supporting experimentation, defining success metrics and contributing to performance reporting. You will provide technical subject matter leadership and guidance to ensure platforms achieve privacy, cyber security and technology best practice, while supporting activities around platform monitoring and technical resolution of incidents and issues. 

 In this role you will help ensure strong delivery discipline across planning cycles, maintain attention to detail in platform implementation and support governance and privacy standards. You will also contribute to improving ways of working across product and engineering to ensure sustainable, high-quality outcomes. 

This is an opportunity to work on high-impact platforms that directly shape how millions of Australians experience and engage with the ABC. 

About You 

You are an established product manager with experience working on CRM, marketing automation or audience engagement platforms. You have hands-on familiarity with tools such as Braze (or similar platforms) and understand how lifecycle communications and segmentation strategies drive audience value. 

You bring strong analytical thinking and are comfortable working with data to inform prioritisation and measure impact. You can translate technical or data-related concepts into clear requirements and collaborate effectively with engineering teams. 

You are organised, detail-oriented and capable of managing multiple workstreams. You build positive working relationships across disciplines and communicate clearly with stakeholders who have varying levels of technical understanding. You're someone who is technically minded and comfortable deep in the detail, but you can also collaborate effectively with business stakeholders to drive audience strategy and outcomes. 

Most importantly, you are motivated by using digital platforms and data to strengthen meaningful audience relationships in a public service context. 

For further information on this position please contact Jamie Nedved, Recruitment Lead via email nedved.jamie@abc.net.au   

We respectfully request that Recruitment Agencies do not submit applications for this position. 

If you require recruitment or selection information in an alternative format, or need adjustments or accessibility support, please email accessibility@abc.net.au 

In line with our focus on diversity, applications are strongly encouraged from Indigenous Australians, people from a range of cultural and linguistic backgrounds, people with disability and LGBTIQA+ individuals. The ABC also aims to achieve a gender-balanced workforce 

For more information on working at the ABC visit  abc.net.au/careers

Company: Sandvik Rock Processing Australia Pty Limited.
Ad Text:
Location: Australia (Sydney, Perth, Brisbane)

Job Type: Full-time, Permanent

Flexibility: Working from Home options in line with Procedure

Closing Date: Applications will close on 15 May 2026 or sooner if a suitable candidate is identified.  




We’re looking for a Business Controller to partner with our Screening Division, providing financial insight that supports strong decisions, sustainable growth, and long‑term value creation.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys turning data into insights, working closely with stakeholders, and influencing outcomes across a global organisation.

As Business Controller, you will play a key role in shaping financial transparency and performance across the Screening Division. By combining strong analytical skills with a collaborative mindset, you’ll help leaders understand what’s happening today and make informed choices for tomorrow.




What you’ll do:

Provide financial insight and analysis to support decision‑making across the Screening Division.

Deliver planning, forecasting, reporting, and performance analysis, including OneStream close activities.

Support investment evaluations, strategic initiatives, and business planning.

Monitor costs, cash flow, and net working capital, identifying improvement opportunities.

Strengthen financial governance and partner with Group and regional stakeholders.

What you’ll bring:

Tertiary qualification in Finance or related field; CPA/CA preferred.

Proven experience in Business Control, FP&A, or similar roles.

Strong analytical skills with a collaborative, commercially minded approach.

Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office and OneStream.

Willingness to travel

Why You’ll Love It Here:

Supportive and Inclusive Culture: Join a team that values collaboration, respect, and diversity.

Career Development: We invest in your professional growth and create opportunities to advance your career.

Work-life Balance:  We understand the importance of flexibility.

Health and Wellbeing: Access to health and wellness programs, including mental health support.

Leadership Support: Learn and connect with experienced professionals who are invested in your success.

Family-Friendly Policies: We offer paid parental leave, and other initiatives to support working parents.

Employee Benefit programs: including discounts program, salary sacrifice options and length of service recognition.




A place where we are all welcome:

At Sandvik, we recognise that we are strengthened by diversity. We are committed to providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer, and we encourage applications from women and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

Sandvik Rock Processing Australia is proud to be a WORK180 Endorsed Employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive and supportive workplace, and you can explore our policies and employee benefits on the WORK180 website.




Who can apply:

This role is open to Australian and New Zealand citizens, Australian permanent residents, and candidates who hold or can obtain a valid working visa.

As we aim for a fair recruitment process, we utilize assessment tools to safeguard objectivity. When you apply for this job, you will therefore receive an invitation via email to a personality and logic ability test. Feedback comes immediately after the test has been completed and the selection process begins afterwards.

As part of our commitment to safety, we may require a national police check and other applicable security, medical, and character requirements for this position.

If you have any concerns, please know that we are here to support you throughout the application process. We wish you the best of luck with your application.




About Sandvik Rock Processing Solutions:

We are a leading provider of eco-efficient rock processing solutions. Leveraging more than 160 years of engineering excellence, we help our customers optimise their comminution and material handling operations for maximum performance, safety, and efficiency.

We apply our profound industry expertise in crushing, screening, feeding, loading and wear protection, to bring an unrivalled equipment line-up to the market. This unique offering is underpinned by our expert process knowledge, full range of digital tools, high-quality OEM spare parts, consumables, and life-cycle services.

Employing around 3000 professionals worldwide, Sandvik Rock Processing Solutions is a business area of the global, high-tech engineering group Sandvik. In 2024, the group had approximately 41,000 employees, sales in more than 150 countries and revenues of about SEK 123 billion.

For more information, please visit https://www.home.sandvik/

ABOUT THE COMPANY.

This leading organisation looking for a Finance Transformation Manager, for a 6 month contract, to lead and deliver strategic finance initiatives that enhance performance, improve processes, and enable scalable growth. This role sits at the intersection of finance, technology, operations and Shared Services driving change across systems, processes, and ways of working.

RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE.

Lead end-to-end finance transformation initiatives, from strategy through to execution
Partner with CFO, Finance Leadership, and key business stakeholders to define and prioritise transformation roadmap
Drive process optimisation across core finance functions
Implement and optimise ERP and finance systems
Improve data quality, reporting capability, and decision support though automation and analytics
Establish best practice financial controls, governance, and standardisation
Lead change management initiatives to embed new processes and systems effectively

SKILLS + EXPERIENCE.

CA/CPA qualified (or equivalent)
Proven experience in finance transformation, ideally within complex or high-growth environments

Experience delivering ERP implementations and finance system enhancements
Commercial mindset with the ability to translate strategy into execution
