Job Title: Manager - Commercial Evaluation & Advisory - 6 month contract
Ad Text:
6 month contract
NSW Government
Experience leading cost model for TM1 production
Experience with IBM Planning Analytics Model
This Manager will be brought on to manage the development of the commercial pricing model for platforms and services that the cluster provides on behalf of whole of government. Additionally, they will take ownership of the practical application of the IBM Planning Analytics model, including the interface with external data sources, to plan, deliver and evaluate output from the model to support achievement of organisational objectives.

Responsibilities

Design, build and evaluate the financial modelling, including scenario analysis, to support funding requests in business cases and cluster submissions to ensure there is consistency across the cluster and strategic financial objectives are met
Lead the excel cost modelling design that will enable TM1 production. Design, build and identify all required external inputs.
Develop, manage and maintain effective customer relationships and partnerships with internal stakeholders (i.e. Finance business partners across the agency)
Developing a sustainable pricing model for the agency across multiple divisions within TM1. Identifying key Cost Centres and understanding how they map to each archetype/product.
How to Apply

If you're ready to take on the next challenge in your career and you think you can bring your passion to this exciting role, then please click on 'Apply Now' to submit your cover letter and resume. For specific questions relating to the role please contract Nick at nraicevich@genesisit.com.au.

Commercial Strategy & Analytics Lead

About AEA
Animal Emergency Australia (AEA) is one of Australia’s leading emergency and critical care veterinary networks, delivering 24/7 clinical services, education, and operational support across a growing national footprint.
We exist to help veterinary professionals do and be their best, while delivering exceptional care to the animals and families who rely on us.
We are a purpose-driven, values-led organisation investing deeply in:

Clinical excellence
Workforce sustainability
Innovation and data-led decision making
Continuous improvement across every team
At AEA, we challenge the heart, support the mind, and operate with integrity, compassion, and kindness in everything we do.
  
Our Values
Kindness | Transparency | Ownership | Teamwork | Growth
These are not aspirational statements, they define how decisions are made and how we operate as a national organisation.
  
The Opportunity
We are seeking a Commercial Strategy & Analytics Lead to define and govern the commercial intelligence that underpins AEA’s strategic and operational decisions.
This is a high-trust, high-impact enterprise role responsible for ensuring the organisation has a single, coherent, and defensible view of commercial performance, growth, workforce economics, and investment decisions.
You will operate as the architect of commercial truth across the organisation.
  
What You Will Own
Enterprise Commercial Intelligence

Long-range (5-10 year) growth and capacity modelling
Hospital and network-level economic frameworks
Capital deployment and expansion modelling
Pricing & Commercial Strategy

Pricing strategy, elasticity, and margin modelling
Scenario analysis for commercial decision-making
Post-implementation pricing performance tracking
Workforce & Labour Economics

Workforce cost modelling and productivity analysis
Remuneration and scalability modelling
Workforce scenario planning in partnership with P&C and Operations
Business Intelligence & Data Governance

Ownership of BI strategy (Power BI, Anaplan)
Executive Data Dictionary and metric governance
Elimination of shadow reporting and inconsistent metrics
Executive Decision Support

Translate complex analysis into clear executive insight
Support Board and Executive decision-making with commercial clarity
Raise organisational decision quality and analytical maturity
  
What Success Looks Like

A single, trusted source of commercial and operational truth
Consistent and governed enterprise metrics
Executives making faster, higher-quality decisions
Clear linkage between data, insight, and strategic action
Reduced ambiguity across finance, operations, and workforce planning
  
About You
You will bring:

5-8+ years in strategy, consulting, FP&A, or commercial analytics
Strong financial modelling capability (scenario and long-range)
Experience in complex, ambiguous, stakeholder-rich environments
Strong Power BI capability (Anaplan highly desirable)
Confidence influencing senior executives with data-led insight
Ability to move between detail and strategic narrative
Highly regarded:

Consulting background (Big 4 or strategy firm)
Healthcare, operations, or multi-site business exposure
Experience building BI frameworks or enterprise data governance
  
Why Join AEA?
At AEA, your work directly shapes how emergency veterinary care is delivered across Australia.
You will:

Influence national growth and investment decisions
Work closely with Executive and operational leadership
Build enterprise-wide analytical capability from the ground up
Be part of a values-led, purpose-driven organisation
Help improve outcomes for animals and the people who care for them
  
Ready to Shape Commercial Intelligence That Matters?
If you want to move beyond reporting and into true enterprise decision enablement—we would love to hear from you.

Apply now or send your CV and Cover letter to Tee at careers@aes.email

Your Next Opportunity Campbell Property Group (CPG) — one of Australia’s leading residential construction and property development companies — is seeking a highly capable Senior Business Analyst to join our Project Management Office (PMO) team in Norwest.

In this role, you will play a key part in driving business transformation initiatives across the organisation. Partnering closely with business leaders, project teams and technology specialists, you will analyse complex business challenges, define clear requirements and help deliver practical solutions that improve processes, systems and outcomes. If you’re an experienced analyst who enjoys turning business needs into well-designed solutions and influencing meaningful change, this is a fantastic opportunity to contribute to strategic initiatives within a leading Australian builder.

What you’ll do

Lead the elicitation, analysis and documentation of functional and non-functional business requirements
Facilitate workshops and stakeholder consultation sessions to gather, validate and refine requirements
Analyse current business processes and identify opportunities for improvement, automation and efficiency
Translate business needs into clear requirements that support effective solution design and delivery
Provide analysis, advice and recommendations to stakeholders on solution options and implementation impacts
Support solution design discussions and participate in testing, including user acceptance testing
Contribute to business cases, benefits realisation planning and project workstream planning
Provide reporting on analysis progress, risks and issues to project leadership and stakeholders

About You

To succeed in this role, you will bring:

Demonstrated experience working as a Business Analyst or Senior Business Analyst, with 3-7 years' experience in business analysis, process improvement, and systems analysis
Proven ability to gather requirements, analyse processes and design practical solutions
Experience working across business and technology teams to deliver system or process improvements
Strong understanding of business analysis frameworks and practices (e.g. BABOK)
Familiarity with Agile, Scrum or hybrid delivery environments
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capability
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, including facilitating workshops
Highly organised and proactive, able to manage multiple priorities and influence outcomes
Experience in the Construction or Property industry highly desirable
IIBA Certification or equivalent also highly regarded

What’s on offer

When you join Campbell Property Group, you’ll enjoy:

Ongoing coaching and mentoring from industry leaders
Health and wellbeing perks – gym discounts, health insurance, annual nutrition & fitness check-ins
Retail discounts across travel, groceries, and lifestyle
Savings on home builds, trades, suppliers, and utilities
Ongoing learning and development through the CPG Academy
Generous leave - including birthday, parental, volunteer and community service leave
Wellbeing support for you and your family (EAP, financial coaching, health programs)

At Campbell Property Group (CPG), we’re passionate about creating better futures, one home at a time.

Our businesses include:

Clarendon Homes – award-winning, innovative homes across QLD and NSW
Domaine Homes – affordable, stylish homes designed for modern families
CPG Estates – developing vibrant, sustainable communities
Loan Market Elevate – expert financial solutions to support our customers’ property journeys

With over 40 years in the home building industry, we’ve built a reputation for quality, integrity, and customer care.

At Campbell Property Group (CPG), everyone has a home. We are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive workplace where everyone feels safe, respected and supported and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

Job Title: Causal Lecturer for Business or Cybersecurity
Ad Text:
AUSTRALIAN INSTITUTE OF BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE (AIBI)

Melbourne Campus | Excellence in Business & Technology Education

Casual Lecturer – Business & Cybersecurity

Melbourne, VIC | Casual / Part-Time | Education & Training




About AIBI

The Australian Institute of Business Intelligence (AIBI) is a leading higher education provider delivering MBA, postgraduate, and undergraduate programs in Business and Cybersecurity. With a campus in the heart of Melbourne, we are committed to equipping students with the knowledge, skills, and industry insights needed to excel in a rapidly evolving global environment. Our faculty comprises experienced academics and industry professionals passionate about delivering high-quality, future-focused education.




The Opportunity

We are expanding our talented team and inviting expressions of interest from experienced academics and industry professionals to join us as Casual Lecturers across our Business and Cybersecurity discipline areas. If you hold a strong educational background, possess contemporary industry experience, and are passionate about empowering students to reach their full potential — this is the opportunity for you!




Discipline Areas – We Are Seeking Lecturers in:

Cybersecurity

Network and Web Security

Data Analytics

Project Management

Enterprise Management Systems

International Strategic Management

Organisational Behaviour and Change Management

Accounting for Business

Quantitative and Qualitative Methods for Business

Digital Business Transformation

International Business

Sales and Logistics Systems




What We Are Looking For

Academic Qualifications: A relevant postgraduate qualification (Master's or PhD preferred) in Business, Cybersecurity, or a closely related field.

Industry Experience: Demonstrated professional experience in your discipline area, with the ability to bring real-world insights into the classroom.

Teaching Excellence: Proven or potential capacity to deliver engaging, high-quality learning experiences at the tertiary level.

Contemporary Knowledge: Up-to-date knowledge of current industry trends, tools, and practices relevant to your discipline.

Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to engage and inspire a diverse student cohort.

Student-Centred Mindset: A genuine commitment to student success and a desire to make a positive difference in learners' academic and professional journeys.




Why Join AIBI?

Work alongside an experienced, collaborative, and dedicated academic team.

Contribute to industry-relevant, future-focused programs in Business and Cybersecurity.

Flexible casual teaching arrangements to suit your professional schedule.

Opportunity to make a meaningful impact on the careers of postgraduate and undergraduate students.

Supportive learning environment in a modern Melbourne campus setting.




How to Apply

If you have a strong academic profile, contemporary discipline knowledge, and demonstrated industry experience, we would love to hear from you. Please submit your application via Seek.com.au, including:

A current curriculum vitae (CV) highlighting your academic qualifications and industry experience.

A cover letter addressing the selection criteria outlined above.

Contact details of two professional referees.




Please note: Only successful candidates will be contacted.

AIBI is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates.

About Us:

We create extraordinary experiences. We create memories, we take care of our shared heritage. This means careers where we have to innovate and always push for better, and where your perspective is always valued. This is a world where we bring together our love for the natural environment and our care for one another to make a real difference. This is a world where you’ll discover something new, every day.

[Support Services] Join our Support Services Team and help drive our evolution, as an innovative, modern sector leader. In a business with a $80 million revenue, and an employee engagement score of 87%, you’ll bring commercial knowledge to an essential community focused, purpose driven organisation that is driven by values.

The Role:

We are seeking a Senior GIS Advisor to lead the development and delivery of SMCT’s enterprise geospatial strategy.

Reporting to the Executive Manager – Business Optimisation, you will be responsible for enhancing GIS platforms, improving spatial data capability and embedding geospatial insights across the organisation. This role will see you partnering with stakeholders across multiple functions to deliver high quality spatial solutions that support planning, operations and customer experience.

Responsibilities:

Lead the development and delivery of SMCT’s enterprise GIS strategy and roadmap
Manage and enhance GIS platforms and spatial data to ensure a reliable, high quality source of truth
Improve mapping, modelling and reporting to support planning, asset management and operations
Integrate GIS with corporate systems to enable data driven insights and decision making
Design and enhance customer facing spatial services, including digital mapping and wayfinding tools
Establish governance frameworks, standards and processes to support data quality and compliance
Partner with stakeholders to provide expert advice, build capability and embed GIS across the organisation
Contribute to system improvements, projects and continuous improvement initiatives

Your Profile – We are looking for someone with:

Strong experience working with GIS platforms such as ESRI ArcGIS or QGIS
Proven capability in spatial data modelling, analysis, visualisation and system integration
Experience improving data quality, accessibility and usability at an enterprise level
Ability to translate business needs into practical geospatial strategies and solutions
Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to influence and collaborate across teams
Excellent analytical, problem solving and communication skills
Experience contributing to system improvement or organisational uplift initiatives
Well organised with the ability to manage priorities and deliver outcomes in a complex environment




What’s on Offer:

We offer a fulfilling career with exceptional personal and professional growth opportunities, along with a comprehensive range of benefits:

Access to Leadership and Development Programs to foster professional growth and advancement.
Employee Health, Wellbeing and Assistance Programs including complimentary onsite physio services.
Flexible working arrangements.
Immediate access to unpaid parental leave and a short waiting period of only three months for paid parental leave, applicable to both full-time and part-time employees.
The opportunity to purchase additional annual leave.
Discounts available at our employee café and onsite florists.

Don’t meet every requirement? We still encourage you to apply – your unique strengths might be just what we need.

We care for our colleagues and the community we serve. To protect one another it is a requirement that all successful candidates complete background checks.

If you have any access requirements which restrict you from fully participating in our recruitment process, please contact recruitment@smct.org.au so that we can discuss necessary adjustments.

We're reviewing applications as they come in and may remove this ad early once the position is filled, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

For more information on Southern Metropolitan Cemeteries Trust, please visit our website: www.smct.org.au.

SMCT is an equal opportunity employer and a Diversity Council Australia Member.

Reporting Manager – Power BI

Pinkenba, QLD | Power BI Leadership | Enterprise Reporting

About BGW Group

BGW Group is a proudly Australian, family‑owned business with a strong reputation in the electrical and plumbing wholesale industries. As we continue to grow, we are investing in modern data and reporting capability to support better decision‑making across the organisation.

An exciting opportunity is available for a Reporting Manager – Power BI to lead enterprise reporting outcomes from our Support Office in Pinkenba.

About the Role

This is a hands‑on leadership role responsible for the delivery, quality, governance, and adoption of enterprise Power BI reporting across BGW Group.

Working closely with business stakeholders and the data platform and engineering teams, you will ensure reporting is clear, trusted, consistent, and focused on driving real business insight and value. This role is reporting‑centric, with responsibility for dashboards, KPIs, and stakeholder outcomes rather than underlying data engineering.

Key Responsibilities

Own the end‑to‑end Power BI reporting lifecycle, from requirements through to production and adoption

Lead the delivery of intuitive, performant dashboards aligned to business decision‑making

Prioritise reporting initiatives based on business value and demand

Establish and maintain reporting standards including visual design, KPI definitions, and documentation

Manage Power BI workspaces, apps, security, and row‑level access

Act as the primary point of contact for reporting demand and stakeholder engagement

Drive Power BI adoption and data literacy through training and support

Lead and mentor the reporting team, setting delivery expectations and quality standards

Collaborate closely with data platform and engineering teams, consuming governed datasets and semantic models

Continuously improve reporting usability, performance, and stakeholder satisfaction

About You

You’re an experienced Reporting or BI leader with strong Power BI expertise and a passion for delivering insights that matter.

Essential Experience

Strong hands‑on experience delivering Power BI reports and dashboards

Proven experience leading or managing a reporting or BI team

Strong understanding of Power BI data modelling for reporting

Experience with report performance optimisation and best‑practice visual design

Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Ability to balance standardised enterprise reporting with self‑service needs

Why Join BGW Group?

Modern riverfront office in Pinkenba with free onsite parking

Free onsite gym and barista‑made coffee daily

Birthday leave and Employee Assistance Program

Discounts on products, banking, health insurance, and superannuation

Supportive, values‑led culture focused on teamwork, integrity, and continuous improvement

If you’re passionate about turning data into insight and leading high‑quality reporting at scale, we’d love to hear from you.

Salary: Annual Salary with some great benefits!
Ad Text:
Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little different. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions.

What you’ll be doing…

As the Delivery Lead – Commercial & Operations, you’ll lead a high-performing squad to deliver critical integration and API capabilities that enable our commercial operations across the network. You’ll also champion an AI-first mindset and way of working within your squad encouraging and demonstrating the adoption of the technology to streamline software delivery processes such as requirements engineering, project tracking, design and code generation.

Sitting at the intersection of business and technology, you’ll be accountable for end-to-end delivery—translating strategy into execution, aligning stakeholders, and ensuring initiatives are delivered on time, within budget, and with real business impact.

This includes…

Leadership & Team Performance

Lead and develop a squad of engineers, fostering a strong, engaged and high-performing team
Create a culture of accountability, continuous improvement and delivery excellence
Demonstrate and encourage an AI-first approach to delivery and be a role model to the squad as to what good looks like in an augmented engineering environment.

Delivery & Execution

Own the end-to-end delivery of integration and API initiatives supporting commercial operations
Manage backlogs, sprint planning and prioritisation in partnership with stakeholders
Proactively identify and remove blockers to keep delivery on track

Commercial & Financial Management

Manage budgets, forecasts and financial performance for your squad
Lead estimation of new initiatives, ensuring clear and realistic delivery plans
Track performance against scope, timeline and budget, providing clear insights to stakeholders

Stakeholder Engagement & Governance

Partner closely with business and technology stakeholders to align priorities and outcomes
Provide clear, concise reporting on delivery progress, risks and financials
Manage change requests to ensure alignment with agreed scope and objectives

Vendor & Resource Management

Oversee vendor relationships, including contracts, performance and service levels
Optimise resource allocation to meet both delivery and financial targets

Platform Health & Operational Support

Ensure integration platforms remain modern, secure and resilient
Act as a senior escalation point for complex production or cyber incidents
Embed DevOps practices, including shared ownership of production support

AI Augmented Delivery Embedment

Champion delivery practices that leverage frontier models to drive efficiency, performance and quality across the delivery lifecycle
Work with your engineering lead to determine and trial generative AI approaches that work within the context of Virgin’s software engineering teams to drive efficiency and quality outcomes

 You will be great in this role if you have…

Degree in IT, Computer Science or related field (or equivalent experience)
Proven experience leading delivery of complex technology initiatives, ideally in integration or backend environments
Strong leadership capability, with experience managing and developing high-performing teams
Solid understanding of Agile delivery (Scaled Agile experience advantageous)
Experience delivering cloud-based, scalable solutions (e.g. AWS, containerisation platforms such as EKS)
Strong commercial acumen, including budgeting, forecasting and vendor management
Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence across all levels of the business
Experience working with managed services and offshore delivery partners
Strong communication, problem solving and facilitation skills
Understanding of modern API and integration practices
Familiarity with ITIL, release management, and data/privacy considerations
Airline or broader commercial experience (desirable)
Experience introducing generative AI technologies and frontier models into the software delivery process

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to):

Discounted flights for you and your loved ones (including $1000 worth of travel credits per year)
A flexible, hybrid work style. Work from home or the office, adapt your location to suit your style and needs
Discounts on travel insurance, lounge membership, car hire, accommodation, and experiences worldwide
Cheaper hospitality, retail, technology, beauty and wellness services
Free counselling and access to a wellbeing app to support your physical, mental, social and financial health
‘Dress for Your Day’ – enjoy the freedom to wear whatever makes you feel great for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you.
Enjoy a prime South Bank head office location with train, busway, shops, dining, gym and parklands all just steps away

When everyone is included, everyone wins

We’re all individuals. And we love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTQIA+ community, and anyone else who wants to join our family.

Ready to apply? We’re ready to hear from you. Apply now.

Data Engineer

Pinkenba, QLD | Microsoft Fabric | Modern Data Platform

About BGW Group

BGW Group is a proudly Australian, family‑owned business with a strong reputation in the electrical and plumbing wholesale industries. As we continue to grow, we are investing in modern data platforms and analytics to support better decision‑making across the business.

Based at our Support Office in Pinkenba, you’ll join a collaborative and supportive environment where people are valued and professional growth is encouraged.

About the Role

We are seeking a Data Engineer to design, build, and operate reliable, scalable data pipelines and data structures on our enterprise analytics platform, with a strong focus on Microsoft Fabric.

You’ll work closely with the Data Platform Manager, analysts, and stakeholders to ensure high‑quality, well‑governed data is available to support Power BI reporting, analytics, and advanced data use cases.

Key Responsibilities

Build and maintain data pipelines using Microsoft Fabric (Data Factory, Dataflows Gen2, Lakehouse)

Ingest and transform data from source systems using ELT patterns

Develop robust data transformations using SQL, notebooks, and Fabric‑native tooling

Create curated data layers and analytical models optimised for reporting and analytics

Build and support governed semantic models for Power BI

Embed data quality controls, validation rules, and reconciliation checks into pipelines

Support data governance requirements including security, privacy, metadata, and lineage

Monitor and support production data pipelines and resolve data or performance issues

Collaborate with analysts, BI developers, and platform leads to continuously improve data capability

About You

You’re a technically strong Data Engineer who enjoys building reliable data foundations and working collaboratively across teams.

Key Skills & Experience

Experience as a Data Engineer or Analytics Engineer

Hands‑on experience with Microsoft Fabric or comparable modern data platforms

Strong SQL skills and experience with data transformations (PySpark / Python desirable)

Solid understanding of Lakehouse and data warehouse concepts

Experience supporting Power BI through datasets and semantic models

Strong attention to detail and a focus on data quality and reliability

Clear communicator with a customer‑focused mindset

Why Work With Us

Modern riverfront office in Pinkenba with free onsite parking

Free onsite gym and barista‑made coffee daily

Birthday leave and Employee Assistance Program

Annual flu vaccinations

Discounts across electrical and plumbing products, banking, health insurance, and super

Supportive, values‑driven culture focused on teamwork, integrity, and innovation

If you enjoy building modern data platforms and want to contribute to a growing Australian business, we’d love to hear from you.

BI Data Manager

Pinkenba, QLD | Microsoft Fabric | Data Architecture Leadership

BGW Group is a proudly Australian, family‑owned business with a strong reputation in the electrical and plumbing wholesale industries. As we continue to grow, we are investing in modern data platforms to support enterprise‑wide analytics and decision‑making.

We have an exciting opportunity for a BI Data Manager to lead and evolve our analytics data platform, based at our Support Office in Pinkenba.

About the Role

This is a hands‑on leadership role responsible for the design, operation, and continuous improvement of BGW Group’s enterprise data platform, with a strong focus on Microsoft Fabric. The role is platform‑centric (not report‑centric) and enables trusted, governed, and scalable analytics across the business.

You will lead the BI data team, own the end‑to‑end data architecture, and work closely with business stakeholders to ensure data is reliable, secure, and fit for purpose.

Key Responsibilities

Own and lead the enterprise BI data platform using Microsoft Fabric

Design and maintain scalable architectures across Lakehouse, Warehouse, and OneLake

Oversee data ingestion, transformation, modelling, and availability

Implement data governance, quality, security, and access controls

Enable self‑service analytics through governed semantic models and data products

Lead and mentor a high‑performing BI data team

Translate business needs into robust platform and data design decisions

Drive continuous improvement, automation, and modernisation initiatives

About You

You are an experienced data platform leader with strong technical capability and a collaborative leadership style.

Essential Experience

Data architecture or data platform leadership experience

Hands‑on experience with Microsoft Fabric or equivalent modern analytics platforms

Strong understanding of data warehousing, Lakehouse architecture, and ELT patterns

Advanced Power BI modelling and performance optimisation

Experience implementing data governance frameworks

Proven people management and leadership experience

Strong SQL skills; Python experience for data transformation




Why Join BGW Group?

Modern riverfront office in Pinkenba with free onsite gym and parking

Free barista‑made coffee daily

Birthday leave and Employee Assistance Program

Discounts on products, banking, health insurance, and superannuation

Supportive, values‑led culture with a strong focus on professional growth

If you’re passionate about building modern data platforms and want to make a meaningful impact in a growing Australian business, we’d love to hear from you.

Position Overview:  

We are seeking a Senior Advisory Specialist to lead end-to-end client engagements, coordinating task allocation and delivering Professional Services across the full customer journey and enablement lifecycle. They partner with PS management, Customer Success, and other stakeholders to advise on best practices, perform QA on junior specialists’ work, and mentor new hires. The mandate is to drive adoption and reduce risk through proven delivery while continuously deepening expertise and developing targeted subject-matter specializations. 

The role is intensely technology-driven, consistently exceeding client expectations on deadlines and deliverables. It also executes and delivers managed services requests, operating as an expert in Diligent solutions and collaborating closely with internal teams to get the job done. 

Key Responsibilities  

Lead engagements independently or with a team and coordinate task allocation. Continue to deliver Professional Services over the course of the entire customer journey and/or product enablement lifecycle.
Assist PS management, CS team, and other internal stakeholders in the different stages of the customer lifecycle.
Leverage Domain, Industry and Product expertise to provide guidance to customers on best practices regarding solution rollout and project execution.
Utilize experience to drive successful adoption and reduce associated risks through the delivery of Professional Services.
Perform Quality Assurance of delivery work performed by Advisory Specialists I and II.
Continue to develop Domain and Product expertise to provide guidance to customers on best practices for solution rollout and project execution through continuous learning and personal development.
Identify specialty areas of Domain and/or Product to become a Subject Matter Expert (SME) and prioritize learning and development of those areas.
Act as a mentor for Data Specialist and Advisory Specialist roles, including being a “buddy” for new hires when assigned.
Support senior management on potential Business Development activities. 

Required Experience/Skills  

5-7 years of experience in an internal or external client-facing or consulting role
Embody Customer First mindset in all undertakings, operating with urgency, excellence, and accountability to customer outcomes and experiences.
Maintains a consistently high-quality level of work ethic including attention to detail, accuracy, and following processes and procedures with stakeholders and customers.
Demonstrate effective time management and organization skills and ability to multi-task in a fast-paced environment
Shows a willingness to problem-solve and take on new challenges
Ability to work as part of a team to deliver in tight timeframes and respond quickly in a constantly changing environment
Passionate about GRC.
Excellent communication skills–including delivery of presentations or workshop
Ability to effectively engage customer personnel in solution discussions to resolve issues regarding GRC requirements and goals
Relevant Professional GRC accreditation (planned, in progress, or obtained). 

Preferred Experience/Skills  

Effectively communicate timelines and manage expectations with the customer and internal stakeholders as they align with the project plan.
Understand Adoption Manager (AM) reporting needs for forecasting and budget tracking. Partner with AMs to ensure projects are delivered within budget and fully (all scoped activities).
Utilize a problem-focused and specific mentality to provide constructive feedback to team members.
Ability to understand and interpret solution design plans enabling delivery of activities and milestones detailed in the plans. 

About Us

Diligent is the AI leader in governance, risk and compliance (GRC) SaaS solutions, helping more than 1 million users and 700,000 board members to clarify risk and elevate governance. The Diligent One Platform gives practitioners, the C-Suite and the board a consolidated view of their entire GRC practice so they can more effectively manage risk, build greater resilience and make better decisions, faster. 

At Diligent, we're building the future with people who think boldly and move fast.  Whether you're designing systems that leverage large language models or part of a team reimaging workflows with AI, you'll help us unlock entirely new ways of working and thinking.  Curiosity is in our DNA, we look for individuals willing to ask the big questions and experiment fearlessly - those who embrace change not as a challenge, but as an opportunity.  The future belongs to those who keep learning, and we are building it together.  At Diligent, you’re not just building the future - you’re an agent of positive change, joining a global community on a mission to make an impact.

Learn more at diligent.com or follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook

What Diligent Offers You 

Creativity is ingrained in our culture. We are innovative collaborators by nature. We thrive in exploring how things can be differently both in our internal processes and to help our clients
We care about our people. Diligent offers a flexible work environment, global days of service, comprehensive health benefits, meeting free days, generous time off policy and wellness programs to name a few
We have teams all over the world. We may be headquartered in New York City, but we have office hubs in Washington D.C., Vancouver, London, Galway, Budapest, Munich, Bengaluru, Singapore, and Sydney.
Diversity is important to us. Growing, maintaining and promoting a diverse team is a top priority for us. We foster and encourage diversity through our Employee Resource Groups and provide access to resources and education to support the education of our team, facilitate dialogue, and foster understanding.

Diligent created the modern governance movement. Our world-changing idea is to empower leaders with the technology, insights and connections they need to drive greater impact and accountability – to lead with purpose. Our employees are passionate, smart, and creative people who not only want to help build the software company of the future, but who want to make the world a more sustainable, equitable and better place. 

Headquartered in New York, Diligent has offices in Washington D.C.,  London, Galway, Budapest, Vancouver, Bengaluru, Munich, Singapore and Sydney.   To foster strong collaboration and connection, this role will follow a hybrid work model. If you are within a commuting distance to one of our Diligent office locations, you will be expected to work onsite at least 50% of the time. We believe that in-person engagement helps drive innovation, teamwork, and a strong sense of community.

We are a drug free workplace. Diligent is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, pregnancy, childbirth, physical disability, mental disability, age, military status, protected veteran status, marital status, registered domestic partner or civil union status, gender (including sex stereotyping and gender identity or expression), medical condition (including, but not limited to, cancer related or HIV/AIDS related), genetic information, or sexual orientation in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Diligent's EEO Policy and Know Your Rights. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, you may contact us at recruitment@diligent.com.

To all recruitment agencies: Diligent does not accept unsolicited agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Diligent employees or any other organization location. Diligent is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to step into a senior advisory leadership role within the Department of Education, working on complex, high-impact initiatives that improve how the system operates and delivers outcomes.

As Director, Business Solutions and Advice, you will lead a small, capable team delivering financial insight, business improvement and strategic advisory services across the Department. The work is varied, often ambiguous and requires both structured thinking and practical delivery.

What You’ll Be Doing

·         Leading advisory engagements from problem definition through to implementation

·         Partnering with senior stakeholders to assess performance and identify opportunities

·         Overseeing financial analysis, modelling and reporting

·         Managing multiple workstreams and delivering high-quality outcomes

·         Building team capability and fostering a collaborative, high-performing environment

What You Bring

·         Experience in consulting, advisory, audit or business improvement

·         Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing capability

·         Ability to operate in complex environments with competing priorities

·         A track record of delivering practical outcomes

·         Leadership experience, including developing teams

·         Government experience is valued but not essential.




Why This Role

·         Work on meaningful challenges within a purpose-driven organisation

·         Influence decision making at a senior level

·         See work through to implementation and impact

·         Gain exposure across a large and complex system

How to Apply

To be considered, please submit:

A current resume

A cover letter outlining your experience against the core competencies and responsibilities detailed in the position description

For a confidential discussion, contact:

Ashleigh McMillan | 07 3305 5800 or email a.mcmillan@ontalent.com.au

We are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and welcome applications from diverse backgrounds.

Applications Close 17th May

Help shape what’s next in data, AI, and advisory within Altis Consulting.   We’re looking for a Principal Consultant to join the team and play a key role in building our Victorian success.  

This is more than a delivery role. We’re looking for someone who can bring strong technical capability, commercial thinking, and a modern advisory mindset to help clients solve real business problems with data. 

Join a growing team that values curiosity, collaboration, and practical innovation. You will be someone who is excited by evolving technology, comfortable navigating ambiguity, and motivated by creating solutions that are not only clever, but sustainable, governed, and valuable in the real world. 

In this role 

You will work closely with clients to understand their challenges, shape solutions, and deliver high-impact outcomes across modern data platforms and AI-enabled environments. 

Your role will include: 

Lead the design and delivery of modern data and analytics solutions 
Partner with clients to translate business problems into practical, scalable strategies 
Provide trusted advisory support, recommendations, and technical leadership 
Work across platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, and Microsoft Fabric 
Support the development of governed, sustainable, and future-ready data solutions 
Mentor other team members, share expertise, and help foster a high-performing, collaborative team environment  
Engage confidently with stakeholders across both technical and non-technical backgrounds  

What you will bring 

We’re looking for someone who combines strong foundational skills with a modern mindset. 

Lets talk experience 

You will bring experience in consulting, advisory, or client-facing delivery roles within data and analytics (No specific industry) 
Strong capability across modern data platforms, particularly Snowflake (essential), Databricks, and/or Fabric (desirable) 
Apply strong data engineering and data modelling capability to build scalable, sustainable, and well-governed solutions that turn complex data into practical business value 
Exposure to AI- solutions, or a clear understanding of how AI can be applied responsibly in enterprise environments, is a bonus.  
The ability to solve real-world business challenges, not just build technical outputs 
Confidence in making recommendations, influencing stakeholders, and leading conversations with clients 
A strong understanding of how to deliver solutions that are scalable, practical, and well governed

Added Bonus 

Previous exposure to modern AI-assisted delivery tools (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, Snowflake Cortex Code or similar) and the judgment to use them responsibly to accelerate delivery while maintaining quality. 

The mindset that matters 

Technical capability is important, but it’s only part of the picture. 

We’re especially interested in people who demonstrate: 

A growth mindset 
Curiosity and a genuine interest in new and emerging technologies 
Resilience and adaptability in changing environments 
Strong collaboration and communication skills 
The confidence to challenge thinking, offer insight, and add value beyond delivery 
A practical, solutions-focused approach 

What’s in it for you 

Meaningful work within data, AI, and business advisory 
The chance to help shape client outcomes, team culture, and market presence 
A collaborative environment that values innovation, learning, and impact 
The opportunity to work with modern platforms and forward-thinking clients 

Your Next Chapter Starts Here

At Altis, we offer more than a job. We offer a career opportunity. You’ll have the tools, resources, and support to build your career, take on interesting projects, and work on challenging problems that make a real impact.

We are high performers who support each other to succeed. Our culture encourages bringing your whole self to work, embracing continuous learning, and fostering growth through diverse, complex projects. You’ll be part of a collaborative, inclusive environment where curiosity is valued, ideas are encouraged, and your contribution matters.


How We Invest in You:

A career pathway with ongoing learning and development
Exposure to varied and challenging projects across industries
A supportive, inclusive culture that values diversity and individuality
Hybrid working model
Competitive salary
A holistic health and wellness program that supports you and your family
Paid parental leave
Reward and recognition program

Let’s Start the Conversation

Apply now! We’d love to hear from you.

At Altis, Seeing Your Career Differently means creating an environment where respect, fairness, growth, and well-being are at the core of how we work. We welcome and celebrate people from all backgrounds, experiences, identities, abilities, and thinking styles, and we are committed to providing equal opportunities for all qualified applicants.

If you require adjustments or support during the application process, please let us know. We are here to ensure you can bring your best self to every stage.

Ad Text:
The Department of Education is seeking a Director, Business Solutions and Advice to lead a high-performing advisory function within the Finance, Procurement and Facilities Branch.
This role sits at the centre of complex, high-impact work, partnering with senior leaders across the Department to improve performance, solve critical challenges and support informed decision making.
You will lead a team delivering financial, analytical and business advisory services across a diverse portfolio of initiatives.

About the Department of Education:  

Working for the Queensland Department of Education means joining an organisation that values its people and promotes leadership and innovation. Be part of an environment that respects professionalism and diversity, offers training and development opportunities and embraces flexible careers and work-life balance. Find out more about working with us.  

Key Highlights:

Lead high-impact work across one of Queensland's largest and most complex departments.
Partner directly with senior leaders to shape decisions and drive improvement.
Deliver outcomes that move beyond analysis into implementation and impact.

About You:

You bring a background in consulting, advisory, audit or business improvement and are comfortable operating in complex environments.

You will demonstrate:

Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing capability at a senior level.
The ability to navigate ambiguity and translate insight into action.
A track record of delivering outcomes, not just recommendations.
Sound judgement and decision-making capability.
Experience leading and developing high-performing teams.
Experience within government or large organisations is highly regarded.

Why Join:

This role offers the opportunity to work on meaningful, system-level challenges within a purpose-driven environment. You will gain broad exposure across financial performance, operational improvement and strategic initiatives while contributing to outcomes that have a tangible and lasting impact.

How to Apply:

Attach a brief resume including contact details for 2 referees (referees should have an understanding of your relevant work history) ;
Attach a 2-page written statement in response to the attached role description.  

To be considered, please click apply and you will be redirected to OnTalent recruitment agency.  For a confidential discussion, contact: Ashleigh McMillan | 07 3305 5800.  

We welcome all applicants to share any support needed to ensure our recruitment process is inclusive.  

Applications remain current for 12 months from the closing date and may be considered for appointment to identical or similar vacancies within the Department.  

Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

Company Description


Empower Australia’s Payments Future with Cuscal 

At Cuscal, your skills drive change and make a real impact. Whether you’re supporting our clients’ customers in our Fraud Operations Contact Centre or delivering innovative solutions in Product Delivery, your contribution helps shape the payments industry’s future. 

Join a team that values collaboration, innovation and excellence. With a focus on professional growth and flexibility, Cuscal is where you can thrive. 

Forward with Cuscal. 

Job Description


We are looking for an Agentic Transformation Lead to join our Product Operations team!  

This exciting and newly created role is responsible for supporting the embedment of the strategic adoption of AI technologies, design and management of Agentic AI agents in the future state Product Operations operating model. 

The role focuses on bridging the technologies into a digital workforce where a team of product operations associates manage Agentic AI Agents. You’ll also work on the design of workflows and agentic AI build and support continuous process improvements, optimising workflows and key business processes to drive the strategic design of the agentic human work system. 

You’ll also make an impact by: 

The role of the Agentic Transformation Lead is to design, implement and drive the optimisation and embedment of AI technologies through: 

Strategy and implementation support for the creation of a digital workforce design blueprint  
Analysis of current and future business processes design using change management principles aligned to the product operations goals to drive efficiencies 
Executing the redesign of manual processes to enhance operational efficiency and productivity through the relevant AI technologies following Proof of Concept (POCs)  
Supporting project initiatives to leverage both Agentic and Robotics for cost efficiencies in the workflow design using process intelligence technologies 
Consider the change impacts to human in the loop design principles to ensure risk controls are considered 
Work with the strategic direction of Cuscal’s enterprise AI strategy, policies and frameworks 
Ensure adherence to process governance framework within Product Operations
Assist with the implementation of operational inefficiencies in existing business processes, improving their effectiveness, enhance the workflows, and optimise performance 
Assist with the analysis, design and working through redesign and reengineering practices using six sigma methodologies to optimise processes to drive efficiency 

Qualifications


What you’ll bring 

You thrive in ambiguity and can solve complex problems. You’ve got excellent communication skills and work productively in a high-pressure environment and are keen to contribute to a dynamic team.

To succeed in this role, you’ll have: 

Implementation of AI to improve workforce design and processes 
Exposure to operations, automation, or customer related functions within financial services  
Strong analytical skills, quickly understanding complex concepts and translating them into actionable insights  
Relevant experience in project and change management analysis, data performance and or business analysis, process improvement, or data analysis  
Experience working in Agile, and knowledge of JIRA  
Understanding of ways of working in scrum 
Proven experience in people leadership and driving high performing teams 
Understanding of Cyber security and IT environments 
Incident management protocols and procedural knowledge (including Business Continuity) 
Strategic thinking and proficiency in change management of behavioural shifts in the workplace 
Workplace & IT policies and frameworks 
Understanding of Microsoft Office Suite, JIRA, Confluence 
Management of people, and delivery of initiatives using industry frameworks (change management and project delivery) 
Sound knowledge of business process modelling and workflow documentation

Additional Information


Why Cuscal? 

At Cuscal, you’ll find a strong, successful company that’s reimagining the future. And our team is right there at the heart of it all.   

Here, you’ll deliver or support interesting, ground-breaking work that has real impact - on Australia’s financial services sector and the millions of customers it serves.  

You’ll innovate alongside skilled, smart, connected teams. 

And you’ll build an impressive, fulfilling career that continues to grow.   

You’ll also enjoy a range of benefits, including: 

Recognition and growth: Celebrate achievements through IGNITE and grow with tailored development opportunities. 
Wellbeing focus: Access initiatives supporting physical, mental, and financial health, plus discounts via ‘Cuscal Advantage.’ 
Diversity and inclusion: Join a workplace that values different perspectives and flexible work arrangements. 

Join us and reimagine the future 

Ready to make an impact? Click APPLY to join Cuscal today. 

If you’re excited about this opportunity, we’d love to explore how you can contribute to our vision for the future. Screening and interviews may occur before the job ad closing date, so don’t wait - apply now. 

Cuscal is an equal opportunity employer committed to an equitable, diverse, and socially inclusive work environment and a positive, barrier-free recruitment process.  

We welcome applicants from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage, people living with a disability, LGBTQIA+ and people from culturally diverse backgrounds to explore career opportunities with Cuscal.   

Note: Cuscal does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruitment agencies or search firms.

Why SoftwareOne?

SoftwareOne and Crayon have come together to form a global, AI-powered software and cloud solutions provider with a bold vision for the future. With a footprint in over 70 countries and a diverse team of 13,000+ professionals, we offer unparalleled opportunities for talent to grow, make an impact, and shape the future of technology. At the heart of our business is our people. We empower our teams to work across borders, innovate fearlessly, and continuously develop their skills through world-class learning and development programs. Whether you're passionate about cloud, software, data, AI, or building meaningful client relationships, you’ll find a place to thrive here. Join us and be part of a purpose-driven culture where your ideas matter, your growth is supported, and your career can go global. 


The role

Passionate about leading high‑impact machine learning initiatives and delivering scalable, production‑ready AI solutions?

Motivated to mentor and grow a team of ML and data engineers while remaining hands‑on with advanced model development and deployment?

Inspired to join a collaborative Data & AI organisation that values innovation, technical excellence, and continuous learning?

As our new Machine Learning Engineer – Team Lead, you will play a pivotal role in leading the design and delivery of machine learning solutions end‑to‑end, while providing technical leadership and mentorship to a small, high‑performing engineering team. You will bridge business objectives with advanced ML techniques, guide architectural decisions, and ensure models are production‑ready, scalable, and continuously improved.

Key responsibilities will include:

Lead and mentor a team of ML and Data Engineers, providing technical guidance, regular feedback, and supporting professional development.
Own the end‑to‑end lifecycle of machine learning initiatives, translating business requirements into well‑defined ML problem statements, technical solutions, and delivery plans.
Design, build, train, and evaluate advanced machine learning models, and contribute to architectural decisions for scalable ML pipelines and cloud‑based deployments (Azure preferred).
Partner with MLOps and DevOps teams to implement CI/CD pipelines, model versioning, monitoring, retraining, and production support best practices.
Collaborate cross‑functionally with Data Engineering, Software Development, Sales & Pre‑Sales, and leadership stakeholders, while staying current with emerging ML technologies and driving adoption where valuable.

What we need to see from you
Proven experience delivering machine learning solutions into production environments.
Strong technical expertise in Python and ML frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Scikit‑learn.
Solid understanding of end‑to‑end ML pipelines, data preprocessing, feature engineering, model evaluation, and monitoring.
Experience working with cloud platforms (Azure preferred), Docker, Kubernetes, and MLOps tooling (e.g., MLflow, Airflow).
Ability to balance technical depth with leadership responsibilities and commercial awareness and having experience working within agile delivery environments and across multiple stakeholder groups

Job Function

Software & Cloud
